text.title: Capacity-Fit Calculator Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules that turn the calculator from a vibes estimate into a defensible sprint-planning input.

rule.statement: Effective capacity MUST be computed as: effective_capacity = sum(per_person(work_days × focus_hours)) − ceremonies_total − pto_total − interrupt_buffer. Skipping any term silently is not allowed. If a term is zero, it is recorded as zero with a one-line note.
rule.rationale: The repeating failure is "we'll just use last sprint's velocity" — which embeds last sprint's interruption pattern as if it were a constant. The deterministic formula forces each term into the open.

rule.statement: Subtractions MUST be applied in this order: PTO (whole-person), ceremonies (fixed slots), interrupt buffer (default 15-20% of remaining). The interrupt buffer is computed on the post-PTO-post-ceremony base, not the raw nominal hours.

rationale.source: Empirical: applying buffer to nominal hours over-allocates because PTO already eliminates entire person-days

rule.rationale: Order matters: 20% of 50h is not 20% of 30h. Wrong order routinely overstates capacity by 10-20%.

rule.statement: The interrupt buffer is mandatory and defaults to 15% (mature team, low support load) or 20% (mixed support + product work). A buffer of 0% is forbidden unless the team has a dedicated separate on-call rotation explicitly excluding the planning members. The buffer is justified in writing if set above 25%.
rule.rationale: Teams measure unplanned work post-hoc at 15-30% even when self-described as "shielded." Pretending it is zero is the dominant source of sprint failure.

rule.statement: The fit ratio committed_velocity_estimate / effective_capacity MUST be classified into three bands: GREEN 0.5-0.85 (commit), YELLOW 0.85-1.0 (trim or accept risk in writing), RED > 1.0 OR < 0.5 (re-plan). The band is the calculator's output, not the raw ratio. PM must record the band in sprint-fit-statement.md.
rule.rationale: Teams that commit consistently in 0.7-0.85 hit their commitments >90% of the time. Teams that consistently commit above 0.9 miss 50%+ of commitments and accumulate debt rapidly.

example.title: Computed example
example.code:
Team: 5 engineers, 2-week sprint (10 working days) focus_hours_per_day: 6 (senior x3), 5 (junior x1), 4 (tech lead x1)
Per-person nominal: 3 x 6 x 10 = 180h 1 x 5 x 10 = 50h 1 x 4 x 10 = 40h total nominal = 270h
PTO: tech lead 2 days off = -8h -> 262h Ceremonies per person: 6h x 5 = 30h -> 232h Interrupt buffer 20%: 0.2 x 232 = 46.4 -> effective_capacity = ~186h
Velocity baseline (rolling 3 sprints): 180h delivered avg Committed this sprint: 200h Fit = 200 / 186 = 1.07 -> RED, must trim
example.note: Concrete numbers, all terms present, fit band explicit.

example.title: Vibe estimate
example.code: "Last sprint we did 200 points. Let's commit to 220 because the new joiner is up to speed."
example.note: No formula, no subtraction, no fit ratio. The "new joiner" effect is invented. Violates r1 and r4.
